# WebPeel — Complete API Reference for AI Agents

> This file contains the full API documentation for WebPeel.
> For a shorter overview, see /llms.txt

## Authentication
All API requests require an API key via header:
```
Authorization: Bearer wp_YOUR_KEY
```
Get a key at https://app.webpeel.dev/keys

## GET /v1/fetch
Fetch any URL as clean content.

Parameters:
- url (required) — URL to fetch
- format — markdown (default), html, text, clean
- render — true to use headless browser (for JavaScript-heavy sites)
- stealth — true to bypass anti-bot protection (auto-enables render)
- wait — milliseconds to wait after page load (render mode)
- screenshot — true to capture screenshot
- fullPage — true for full-page screenshot
- selector — CSS selector to extract specific element
- exclude — CSS selectors to exclude
- includeTags — only include these HTML elements
- excludeTags — remove these HTML elements
- raw — true to skip smart extraction, return full page
- noDomainApi — true to bypass domain-specific API extractors
- schema — built-in schema name OR custom JSON schema
- maxTokens — maximum output tokens (truncates intelligently)
- timeout — request timeout in ms (default 30000)

Built-in schemas: product, article, listing, contact, event, recipe, job, business, review

Example:
```
GET /v1/fetch?url=https://amazon.com/dp/B0D1XD1ZV3&schema=product
```

## POST /v1/fetch
Same as GET but with JSON body. Additional options:
- actions — array of page actions [{type:"click",selector:".btn"},{type:"scroll",direction:"down"}]
- extract — {schema: {...}, prompt: "..."} for LLM extraction
- llmProvider — openai, anthropic, google (for LLM extraction)
- llmApiKey — your LLM API key (for LLM extraction)

## GET /v1/search
Web search with structured results.

Parameters:
- q (required) — search query
- limit — number of results (default 10, max 20)
- fetchContent — true to fetch full content for each result
- lang — language code (default en)
- region — region code (default us)

## POST /v1/search/smart
AI-powered smart search with intent detection.

Body: { q: "search query", stream: true/false }

Intent types detected automatically:
- restaurants — Yelp + Google Places + Reddit + YouTube + AI
- products — Amazon/Walmart/Best Buy + Reddit + AI
- cars — Cars.com + web search + AI
- flights — flight aggregators + AI
- hotels — hotel sites + AI
- rental — car rental aggregators + AI
- general — web search + peel top 5 + AI

SSE streaming (stream: true):
Events: intent, progress, source, result, answer, done, error

## GET /v1/feed
Discover and parse RSS/Atom feeds.

Parameters:
- url (required) — website URL or direct feed URL
- limit — max items (default 20, max 100)
- format — json (default) or markdown

## GET /v1/screenshot
Capture webpage screenshots.

Parameters:
- url (required)
- fullPage — true for full page
- width — viewport width (default 1280)
- height — viewport height (default 720)

## POST /v1/crawl
Crawl entire sites.

Body: { url, limit, maxDepth, include, exclude }

## GET /v1/map
Discover all URLs on a site.

Parameters: url, limit

## CLI Reference

```bash
# Fetch
webpeel fetch <url> [options]
  --render          Use headless browser
  --stealth         Anti-bot bypass
  --screenshot      Capture screenshot
  --schema <name>   Extract structured data
  --skip-domain-api Bypass domain extractors
  --raw             Full page, no extraction
  --json            JSON output
  --silent          No progress output

# Search
webpeel search <query> [options]
  --limit <n>       Number of results
  --fetch           Fetch content for each result

# Watch
webpeel watch <url> [options]
  --interval <sec>  Check interval
  --assert <expr>   Alert condition

# MCP Server
webpeel mcp
  Starts MCP server for Claude/Cursor/VS Code
```

## Browser Actions Reference

Actions are passed via --action flag (CLI) or actions array (API).
Each action is a string in format "type:argument".

### Action Types:
- scroll:bottom — Scroll to page bottom
- scroll:top — Scroll to page top
- scroll:down:N — Scroll down N pixels
- scroll:up:N — Scroll up N pixels
- scroll:X,Y — Scroll to coordinates
- wait:N — Wait N milliseconds
- click:SELECTOR — Click a CSS selector
- type:SELECTOR:TEXT — Type text into element
- waitFor:SELECTOR — Wait for selector to appear (10s timeout)
- hover:SELECTOR — Hover over element

### Common Patterns:
# Load infinite scroll content
--action 'scroll:bottom' --action 'wait:2000'

# Click through pagination
--action 'click:.next-page' --action 'wait:1000'

# Fill and submit form
--action 'type:#search:query' --action 'click:.submit' --action 'wait:2000'

# Wait for SPA route change
--action 'wait:3000'

### API Usage:
POST /v1/fetch
{
  "url": "https://example.com",
  "render": true,
  "actions": [
    {"type": "scroll", "direction": "down", "amount": 5000},
    {"type": "wait", "ms": 2000}
  ]
}

## 55+ Domain Extractors
Automatic structured extraction for:
Amazon, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Hacker News, Wikipedia, NPM, PyPI,
Stack Overflow, Medium, Substack, Dev.to, Twitter/X, Instagram, TikTok,
LinkedIn, Pinterest, Spotify, SoundCloud, Twitch, IMDb, BBC, CNN, NYT,
Yelp, Zillow, Redfin, Cars.com, eBay, Etsy, Walmart, Best Buy,
Polymarket, Kalshi, TradingView, CoinGecko, CoinMarketCap, ESPN,
DraftKings, FanDuel, Semantic Scholar, PubMed, ArXiv, AllRecipes,
Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace, ProductHunt, AccuWeather, Weather.com,
Booking.com, and more.

## Tiered Pricing per Request
- simple (HTTP fetch): $0.0002/request
- domain-api (API extractor): $0.0002/request
- stealth (headless browser): $0.005/request
- captcha (anti-bot bypass): $0.02/request

Method is returned in X-Method header and response body.

## Troubleshooting for AI Agents

### Content is sparse or wrong
1. Try --render for JavaScript-heavy sites
2. Try --stealth if the site blocks bots
3. Try --action 'wait:3000' if the SPA loads slowly
4. Try --skip-domain-api to bypass our extractor and get raw page content
5. Check if the URL has client-side routing (e.g. /profile/user on an SPA)

### Screenshot fails
1. Run: npx playwright install chromium
2. The browser binary auto-installs on first use, but may fail in restricted environments
3. Use the API instead: POST /v1/screenshot

### Domain extractor returns wrong page
1. Our extractors handle specific URL patterns. Unknown paths fall through to browser rendering.
2. If you get homepage data for a sub-page URL, add --render to force browser rendering.
3. Use --skip-domain-api to always bypass the extractor.

### Rate limiting
- Free tier: 2,000 requests/month
- API returns 429 with Retry-After header when rate limited
- Use --no-cache to bypass cache, but this counts toward limits
